pattern: Add two reverse-stored digit lists position by position, propagating a single carry into the next node and emitting a final carry digit when it remains.
hints: The digits are stored least-significant-first, so the two heads already line up — add them place by place from the front, just like adding on paper but starting at the ones column.
Carry only one value forward: each step's sum is left-digit + right-digit + carry; the new node holds sum mod 10 and the carry becomes sum divided by 10.
Keep walking while either list has nodes left or the carry is nonzero; a dummy head lets you append uniformly and return dummy.next.

edge_inputs.description: carry cascades through every position and produces a new leading digit
addition that creates one extra high-order node (single carry out)
carry rippling but ending without an extra node

stress.description: two long random digit lists, lengths near the cap, exercising sustained carries
stress.note: no leading zero except the single-node value 0

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def addTwoNumbers(self, l1, l2):
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        tail = dummy
        carry = 0
        while l1 or l2 or carry:
            s = carry
            if l1:
                s += l1.val
                l1 = l1.next
            if l2:
                s += l2.val
                l2 = l2.next
            carry, digit = divmod(s, 10)
            tail.next = ListNode(digit)
            tail = tail.next
        return dummy.next
